personal.name: Sai Krishna Bykani
personal.title: QA Automation Engineer / SDET
personal.summary: Software Development Engineer in Test (SDET) with 3 years of experience in test automation, API testing, performance testing, and backend validation for payment, UPI, and transaction-driven systems. Strong expertise in Java, Selenium WebDriver, Cucumber BDD, REST Assured, Postman, Newman CLI, and Apache JMeter. Proven experience in end-to-end test automation, API integration testing, log analysis, and defect lifecycle management. Adept at working in Agile/Scrum environments, collaborating with developers, and ensuring release quality, system stability, and production readiness.

experience.description: Driving end-to-end software quality assurance for high-volume Fintech and transaction applications.
bulletPoints: Worked on functional, regression, integration, and end-to-end testing for web and backend payment applications.
Developed and maintained UI automation scripts using Selenium WebDriver, Java, Cucumber (BDD), and TestNG.
Automated critical payment and transaction workflows, improving regression coverage and execution efficiency.
Built and executed API automation tests using REST Assured, Postman, and Newman, validating request/response payloads and business rules.
Performed performance and load testing using Apache JMeter for UPI and card transaction flows.
Configured and executed distributed JMeter master–slave setup for concurrency and scalability testing.
Conducted backend data validation using MySQL, Azure SQL, and MongoDB to ensure data consistency across UI, API, and backend systems.
Analyzed application logs and Grafana dashboards to identify performance issues and SLA risks.
Supported CI/CD pipelines by integrating automation suites with Jenkins for scheduled and release-based execution.
Logged, tracked, and managed defects using Jira, Azure DevOps, and Spira, including root cause analysis and fix validation.

projects.title: Payment Gateway Automation
projects.brief: End-to-end automation of merchant onboarding and card transaction flows with 40% regression stability improvement.
projects.description: A comprehensive automation suite designed to validate complex payment processing systems, merchant onboarding portals, and transactional flows. The framework simulates active user transactions and validates transaction success criteria across UI inputs and API responses.
projects.problem: Merchant onboarding and core transaction flows were previously manual, taking hours to execute during releases and leading to occasional regression leaks in merchant checkout redirects.
projects.context: Fintech payment processing requires 100% correctness. Regressions in transaction processing can lead to immediate financial impact and loss of user trust.
projects.results: Decreased production bugs by 80% through shifting quality left and verifying redirects across multiple banks, payment links, and card types.
projects.challenges: Handling third-party 3D-secure OTP simulator screens and checking asynchronous webhook updates on merchant databases.
projects.solution: Built dynamic polling services using Awaitility and decoupled OTP simulators using sandbox mock service integrations.
projects.frameworkDesign: Page Object Model structure with separate directories for Step Definitions, Page Objects, Runners, and Utility classes.
projects.folderStructure:
src/
├── main/java/
│   ├── pages/
│   │   ├── LoginPage.java
│   │   └── PaymentPage.java
│   └── utils/
│       ├── DriverFactory.java
│       └── DBUtils.java
└── test/
    ├── java/
    │   ├── runners/
    │   │   └── TestRunner.java
    │   └── stepDefinitions/
    │       └── PaymentSteps.java
    └── resources/
        └── features/
            └── PaymentGateway.feature

projects.title: Distributed Performance Testing
projects.brief: Distributed load testing of card and UPI transaction flows under 3x peak load conditions.
projects.description: Performance verification framework designed to load test high-concurrency payment pathways. Utilizes a distributed master-slave JMeter architecture deployed in cloud and on-prem environments.
projects.problem: Unpredicted UPI load spikes during promotional sales caused API response latencies to exceed SLA targets (2s) on the bank gateways.
projects.context: UPI transaction pipelines process thousands of concurrent requests. Under high concurrency, system latencies must remain low, and database locks must be avoided.
projects.results: Identified critical connection pool limitations in backend databases, preventing connection timeout crashes under load.
projects.challenges: Synthesizing distributed load injection without causing CPU bottlenecks on the master injector node.
projects.solution: Set up a JMeter master-slave pipeline on lightweight Linux containers, feeding telemetry metrics directly to InfluxDB and visualizing load in Grafana.
projects.frameworkDesign: Distributed master injector coordinating load generators, executing parameterized .jmx script scenarios.
projects.folderStructure:
jmeter/
├── config/
│   ├── user.properties
│   └── system.properties
├── scripts/
│   ├── UPI_Load_Test.jmx
│   └── Card_Load_Test.jmx
└── data/
    ├── cards_dataset.csv
    └── merchants_dataset.csv

projects.title: API Automation Framework
projects.brief: Robust REST Assured automation framework for payment webhooks, transaction status API checks, and merchant ledger validations.
projects.description: An enterprise-grade, clean API test framework built in Java with REST Assured. Integrates deep assertion tools, schema validation, and dynamic payload builders for verifying critical financial endpoints.
projects.problem: Microservice APIs were being modified without proper backward compatibility checks, leading to integration issues during weekly deploys.
projects.context: Payment ledger systems, payouts, and bank settlement APIs require strict contract verification to ensure zero variance in ledger reporting.
projects.results: Achieved continuous regression runs on every build, preventing deployment of incompatible API changes to staging environments.
projects.challenges: Managing dynamic API signatures and encrypting payment payload headers (such as HMAC tokens) in real time.
projects.solution: Built a custom RequestSpecBuilder interceptor that automatically computes SHA-256 HMAC checksum headers for every request.
projects.frameworkDesign: Base API service class, payload factory using Jackson serialization, and descriptive test files using TestNG assertions.
projects.folderStructure:
src/
├── main/java/
│   ├── api/
│   │   ├── BaseAPI.java
│   │   ├── PayloadFactory.java
│   │   └── HMACGenerator.java
│   └── models/
│       ├── TransactionRequest.java
│       └── TransactionResponse.java
└── test/java/
    └── tests/
        ├── TransactionAPITest.java
        └── LedgerAPITest.java

projects.title: Backend Validation & Monitoring
projects.brief: Automated data integrity checks verifying transactions across MySQL, MongoDB, and Grafana panels.
projects.description: A validation pipeline designed to check that when a transaction is completed, records are written consistently in SQL ledger databases and MongoDB document vaults, and system health is reporting correctly in Grafana.
projects.problem: Occasional network disruptions led to ledger records mismatches where transactions succeeded in the gateway but failed to reflect in DB records.
projects.context: Ledger consistency is critical. Data must match across UI, API, and the DB for audits.
projects.results: Established automatic reconciliation, reducing manual verification work by the operations team by 90%.
projects.challenges: Validating unstructured Mongo collections and executing quick cross-database joins.
projects.solution: Built a unified validation engine utilizing MongoDB Java client alongside JDBC, mapping rows to common Transaction models.
projects.frameworkDesign: Database client utility wrappers, data comparative assertions, and Grafana alert query scripts.
projects.folderStructure:
src/
├── main/java/
│   └── database/
│       ├── MySQLClient.java
│       ├── MongoClient.java
│       └── DataValidator.java
└── test/java/
    └── dbtests/
        ├── LedgerReconciliationTest.java
        └── AuditLogTest.java

achievements: Recognised for automation testing and API validation contributions that improved regression stability by ~40% and increased automated test coverage across critical payment flows.
Played a key role in performance and load testing of high-volume payment and UPI transaction flows using Apache JMeter, validating system behaviour under 2x–3x peak load conditions.
Supported successful release sign-offs by ensuring end-to-end test coverage across UI automation, API testing, backend validation, and integration testing, reducing post-release defects and rework.

certifications.name: REST API Test Automation with REST Assured
